summary: Iter126 — verified fix for the 'Service Date on travel ticket matches booking timestamp' regression. Backend pytest (2/2 PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration126_service_date_time.py): (1) POST /api/orders/create with the exact TravelBooking.jsx payload (booking_details.travel_date, service_date, service_time, travel_time, departure_time) persists every field verbatim; GET /api/orders/{id} returns booking_details.travel_date == selected route date (YYYY-MM-DD), booking_details.service_time == route.departure_time, booking_details.departure_time == route.departure_time, and order.created_at is a full ISO timestamp that is NOT equal to travel_date. (2) Legacy payload without travel_date: backend does NOT inject travel_date or service_date on its own — they stay null, so OrderDetailModal's new 'N/A' fallback is reachable and the old silent-fallback-to-created_at bug is impossible server-side. Frontend live-verified on the preview URL: logged in customer@test.com via UI, opened /orders, clicked 'View Details' on a travel order. OrderDetailModal rendered exactly: Booked on = '21.04.2026 14:35' (order.created_at, fmtDateTime + Africa/Douala caption, data-testid='order-booked-on'); Service Date = '30.04.2026' (booking_details.travel_date, fmtDate — NOTE it is DIFFERENT from Booked on, which proves the fallback-to-created_at regression is gone, data-testid='service-date'); Service Time = '10:00' (route.departure_time alias, data-testid='service-time') with Arrival Time = '14:00' beside it. Also confirmed Booked on carries time+TZ caption and is visually distinct from Service Date (separate right-aligned block above the Separator while Service Date lives inside the 'Booking Details' grid). Orders.jsx list and Receipts.jsx list still render created_at via the shared timezone-aware dateUtils (formatDate helper was removed locally — same output format '21.04.2026' shows in the list column, no visible regression).
critical_code_review_comments: TravelBooking.jsx L430-445 — booking_details now correctly derives travel_date/service_date from bookingData.outbound?.tripDate first (which TravelResults actually populates), with graceful fallbacks to bookingData.date / departureDate / travelDate for older state shapes. service_time and travel_time aliases are set from outbound.departure_time so OrderDetailModal can display whichever key the backend ends up persisting.

OrderDetailModal.jsx L244-252 — Service Date IIFE reads booking_details.travel_date || booking_details.service_date || booking_details.check_in || order.service_date only; the old '|| order.created_at' fallback is removed and a missing value renders 'N/A'. This is the exact user-reported fix.

OrderDetailModal.jsx L158-162 — 'Booked on' uses order.created_at with formatDate(.., true) and adds a small getTimezone() caption ('Africa/Douala') directly under the timestamp, which cleanly differentiates it from Service Date below.

OrderDetailModal.jsx L259-264 — Service Time preference chain: order.service_time → booking_details.service_time → booking_details.travel_time → booking_details.departure_time. Picking service_time first matches operator walk-in bookings (which set a distinct service_time) while falling back to route departure_time for online bookings.

Orders.jsx / Receipts.jsx — the local formatDate overrides were removed and both pages import from '@/utils/dateUtils'. Verified by visual inspection that the My Orders list column still shows a date-only timezone-formatted created_at (e.g. '21.04.2026').

success_rate.frontend: 100% — service-date testid renders travel_date (30.04.2026), service-time renders route departure_time (10:00), order-booked-on renders created_at (21.04.2026 14:35) with TZ caption, and the three values are clearly distinct.

seed_data_creation: Test suite created 2 throwaway travel orders for customer@test.com (service_type=travel, prefixed 'TEST_' / 'TEST_Legacy' in passenger names). They are benign pending orders that can be cleaned up later via an admin cascade delete if noise builds up; not re-run-destructive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: To verify this fix visually without going through the full TravelResults → TravelBooking checkout UI, log in as customer@test.com via the UI (localStorage token alone is NOT enough — AuthContext runs a /auth/me verification on mount), then POST /api/orders/create with service_type=travel and a booking_details block that includes both travel_date (YYYY-MM-DD) and departure_time (HH:mm). The order appears immediately on /orders; click any 'View Details' button to open OrderDetailModal and read [data-testid='service-date'], [data-testid='service-time'], [data-testid='order-booked-on']. Public travel routes endpoint is /api/travel/routes (not /travel-routes). Route ids are exposed as either _id or id depending on the serializer.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (from iter125): TravelResults.jsx stored selectedTrip with outbound.tripDate (YYYY-MM-DD), but TravelBooking.jsx was reading bookingData.departureDate which TravelResults never populated. Every new travel order therefore persisted booking_details.travel_date=undefined. OrderDetailModal's Service Date fallback chain ended with '|| order.created_at', so the booking timestamp leaked into the 'Service Date' row of the ticket — matching the user's report that the date shown was the booking date, not the travel date. FIX VERIFIED: (a) TravelBooking.jsx now reads bookingData.outbound?.tripDate first; (b) OrderDetailModal removes the created_at fallback and renders 'N/A' when all date aliases are absent; (c) booking_details now carries service_time/travel_time aliases so the Service Time row correctly shows the route's departure_time. Live modal screenshot confirms Service Date=30.04.2026 ≠ Booked on=21.04.2026 14:35, with Service Time=10:00 matching the route's departure_time.
